requestSummary.userRequest: Align the Tag Param and Active Param edit control left edges with their header text left edges inside the SMART-51 and SMART-52 Laminator Tag tabs.
requestSummary.targetSlice: SMART-51 and SMART-52 Laminator Tag tab layout inside the WindowsUtility legacy portfolio
requestSummary.writeBoundary: layout-only CardPrinterConfig resource coordinate change; broader WindowsUtility portfolio context is read-only

projectMemorySummary.nonAuthorityWarning: Project Memory is displayed as persistent preview context only. It does not change graph selection, traversal, contract input, instruction packs, approval, apply, Evidence, equivalence, scope, or CI authority.

nodes.label: windowsutility-legacy
nodes.intentClaim: Utility_Windows is the legacy C++ utility portfolio that DevView should retrofit as a graph before bounded work is compiled.

nodes.label: windowsutility-integrated
nodes.intentClaim: WindowsUtility is the newer integrated utility workspace that can receive future migration and parity context after legacy slices are mapped.

nodes.label: cardprinterconfig
nodes.intentClaim: CardPrinterConfig is the currently detailed retrofit slice with graph-backed change records and instruction pack context.

nodes.label: config
nodes.intentClaim: WindowsUtility.Modules.Config is an integrated target area for future configuration utility parity.

nodes.label: diagnostics

nodes.label: dump
nodes.intentClaim: WindowsUtility.Modules.Dump and DumpView are integrated target areas for future dump utility parity.

nodes.label: printerinfo
nodes.intentClaim: WindowsUtility.Modules.PrinterInfo and SimpleInfo are integrated target areas for future printer info parity.

nodes.label: cardprinterconfig
nodes.intentClaim: CardPrinterConfig is a Windows MFC configuration utility for SMART printer devices and attached modules.

nodes.label: smart51-printer
nodes.intentClaim: SMART-51 printer-body configuration owns the Factory GetConfig path used by the validated-then-reverted test display.

nodes.label: smart51-laminator
nodes.intentClaim: SMART-51 Laminator owns one of the Tag tab layouts in the active layout-only change.

nodes.label: smart52-laminator
nodes.intentClaim: SMART-52 Laminator owns the matching Tag tab layout in the active layout-only change.

nodes.label: smart51-getconfig-index-000
nodes.intentClaim: Read SMART-51 Factory index 000 during real GetConfig and display it without Set/save/load participation.

nodes.label: laminator-tag-param-columns
nodes.intentClaim: Tag Param and Active Param edit controls align their left edges to their matching header text in SMART-51/52 Laminator Tag tabs.

nodes.label: smart51-test-read-only
nodes.intentClaim: The SMART-51 test display must not participate in Set, save/load, report/export, or unrelated module behavior.
codeLocations: No logic, read/write, save/load, or hardware behavior change.
No unrelated model or module changes.

nodes.label: laminator-layout-only
nodes.intentClaim: The Laminator Tag tab change is layout-only and must not modify C++ logic, resource IDs, device I/O, or save/load behavior.
codeLocations: No logic, read/write, save/load, or hardware behavior change.
No unrelated model or module changes.

nodes.label: smart51-test-setting
nodes.intentClaim: A bounded hardware-validated SMART-51 test display was implemented, confirmed, then reverted by user request.

nodes.label: laminator-tag-layout
nodes.intentClaim: A bounded resource-only Laminator Tag tab alignment fix remains active after build and user UI confirmation.

edges.claim: CardPrinterConfig is one utility inside the Utility_Windows legacy portfolio.
CardPrinterConfig is the only portfolio module currently expanded into detailed retrofit graph context.
CardPrinterDiagnostics is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterFirmware is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterLCD is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterLock is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterModel is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterModel2 is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterRibbon is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterSubEncoder is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
CardPrinterTest is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
NetAdmin is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SimplePrinterInfo is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SimplePrinterInfo2 is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SM8Serial is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SMART51_CmdTool is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SmartComm2 is observed as a shared communication layer candidate.
SmartDump is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SmartDumpView is observed as a legacy portfolio module.
SmartPrinterLib is observed as a shared library candidate.
WindowsUtility integrated Config module is a future parity target for selected legacy configuration utilities.
WindowsUtility Diagnostics is a future parity target for diagnostics utilities.
WindowsUtility Dump modules are future parity targets for dump utilities.
WindowsUtility PrinterInfo modules are future parity targets for printer information utilities.
The SMART-51 printer-body module is a bounded domain slice under CardPrinterConfig.
The SMART-51 printer-body GetConfig flow is the only approved source for the test display value.
The change was allowed only as a read-only projection of index 000 during GetConfig.
Future maintenance must not resurrect the reverted test field through Set/save/load/report or unrelated module paths.
The SMART-51 Laminator Tag tab is one half of the layout-only alignment slice.
The SMART-52 Laminator Tag tab is the matching half of the layout-only alignment slice.
SMART-51 Laminator uses the Tag Param and Active Param column layout surface.
SMART-52 Laminator uses the same Tag Param and Active Param column layout surface.
The active change is driven only by visual left-edge alignment of existing edit controls.
Future maintenance must keep this slice resource-layout-only unless the user explicitly opens logic or hardware scope.

nonExecutionBoundary: DevViewGraph renders a read-only HTML/data inspector from graph-source and instruction-pack artifacts. It does not trigger Codex execution, mutate graph-source, apply graph deltas, approve work, record human decisions, satisfy runtime Evidence, prove equivalence, enforce scope, or configure CI.
